Hey Dan,


Saw your job post, liked it and decided to apply.


I've got some info on my linkedin if you have time to check it out. Otherwise my resume is likely getting parsed in your ATS system and we'll see how things go that way.


Anyway if you see some alignment and are still looking feel free to reach out.


Kind regards,


I am currently a contractor over on the Global Real Estate team doing process improvements, and I am really enjoying the culture and the scope of the work we do here. I saw your posting for the Sr Program Manager position (Job ID: 3177400) and wanted to reach out to express my interest in transitioning to a full-time role.
In my current capacity, my work focuses on PMO and process improvement across the full real estate lifecycle. I handle both internal and external focused process improvements conducting interviews focused on pinpointing pain points and bottlenecks around our org. Recently, I've been engineering Agentic AI solutions and utilizing JavaScript to automate administrative bottlenecks, (Think extraction and autofill) recovering thousands of hours of capacity for our transaction managers while improving visibility for our external partners strengthening those relationships.
Because I am currently a contractor, could you clarify the best application path for this role? I know as a contractor I don't have access to the internal "Request Informational" workflow, but I am not sure how far those restrictions extend. Do contractors qualify to apply for these internal roles, or is there a specific pipeline for active contractors looking to convert to full-time?
Thank you in advance for your time and guidance. I have attached my resume for your review and would love to stay on your radar for this or future opportunities that you may be working to fill.


-
I am currently a contractor over on the Global Real Estate team doing process improvements, and I am really enjoying the culture and the scope of the work we do here. I saw your posting for the Program Manager III – MBA position (Job ID: AMZ24900.1) and wanted to reach out to express my interest.
In my current capacity, my work focuses on PMO and organizational enablement. I lead user listening initiatives and conduct interviews to identify systemic bottlenecks and improve workflow effectiveness based on direct feedback. Recently, I've been utilizing Agentic AI to automate the synthesis of these feedback loops and track team responsibilities, recovering thousands of hours of capacity for our org while providing a transparent framework for continuous process improvement.
I have attached my resume for your review and would love to stay on your radar. I am very interested in the work your team is doing and would appreciate the opportunity to connect if you see a fit.
Hi Becca,
I saw your listing for the Program Manager in AI Operations role on the internal job board and wanted to reach out and introduce myself.
I’m currently contracting over on the Global Real Estate Team  owing tons of process improvements, I’m hoping to transition into a full-time role eventually. Would be very interested in learning more about the team and the role if you feel there is alignment. 
In my current role, I've been heavily focused on AI workflow architecture, building out predictive analytics, creating AI-enhanced SOPs, and using Atlas AI to help automate some of our larger operational bottlenecks. These updates have been embraced by the team and some others have some eyes on them by various tech teams looking to scale our team’s fixes to support a much larger section of the company. I love this work and would love to do it full time if given the opportunity. 
I know you are likely incredibly busy, but if you have 10 or 15 minutes in the next week or two, I'd love to grab a quick virtual coffee to learn more about your vision for the team and see if my background might be a good fit for what you're looking for.
Either way, hope you're having a great week!
Best,
Jeremy Spaunhurst
